sections.title: Business need & success metrics
sections.content:
We are consolidating the British Gas smart-meter data ingestion (~14 billion meter reads / month from 11.6m SMETS2 endpoints) from a single Slough facility into four regional Tier III+ edge data centres. Cooling underpins our ability to meet the Smart Energy Code data-availability obligation to Ofgem and is the single largest lever for our 2030 Scope 2 commitment.

**Success metrics (12 months post-go-live):**
1. Zero cooling-driven outages of the SMETS2 ingestion platform.
2. Operational PUE ≤ 1.28 (design point 1.25 + measurement margin) across all four sites.
3. ≥ 4,200 free-cooling hours/year achieved per site (measured by BMS).

comments.text: Thanks Mark — locking this in. I'll mirror the success metrics into the evaluation framework so suppliers know what 'good' looks like.

sections.title: Performance requirements (PUE, redundancy, free cooling)
sections.content:
**Mandatory:**
- Tier III+ uptime certification of CRAH unit families bid (Uptime Institute or TIA-942 evidence).
- N+1 redundancy on every cooling subsystem — chilled water plant, CRAH, pumps, controls.
- PUE design point ≤ 1.25 at full IT load, ASHRAE A1 envelope, UK climate.
- Water-side economiser or equivalent free cooling active for ≥ 4,200 hours/year (UK Met Office TMY3).
- F-Gas Regulation compliance — refrigerants with GWP ≤ 675 at award; phase-down to GWP ≤ 150 by 31 December 2030.

**Preferred:**
- N+2 chiller redundancy where the cluster CapEx delta is < 8%.
- AI-driven setpoint optimisation with ≥ 12 months of customer-evidenced energy savings.

comments.text: Tightened the GWP threshold to 675 to match the 2027 F-Gas tightening — caught this in the agent's draft.

sections.title: Acceptance criteria & commissioning evidence
sections.content:
**Draft for Mark + Neil to confirm:**

Each site shall be considered Accepted upon:
1. Successful Integrated Systems Test (IST) demonstrating N+1 chiller failover under full IT load with no temperature excursion above ASHRAE A1.
2. 14-day burn-in at ≥ 80% of design IT load with no critical alarm (Severity 1 or 2).
3. Provision of a Commissioning Evidence Pack including: as-built drawings, BMS point list, refrigerant log, control sequence of operations, training register (minimum 24 hours per site).
4. Independent peer review of the IST report by a third party from Centrica's pre-approved list.
5. Sign-off by the Centrica site authority (Critical-Facilities Engineer) and the Mitie FM partner shift lead.

ai_questions.text: Mark — for Item 4 I've left 'pre-approved list' as a placeholder. Do you want me to seed it with the three names from the Hams Hall refresh (Arup, WSP, Cundall) or leave it open?
Neil — should the 14-day burn-in be at 80% IT load (current draft) or 100% as the Mitie team suggested in the workshop on April 14th?

sections.title: Commercial framework & terms
sections.content:
**Pricing model:** Fixed-price CapEx per cluster + 10-year OpEx schedule (preventive + reactive + consumables) with annual indexation capped at CPI − 1%.
**Payment terms:** Net 60 from valid invoice (Centrica standard).
**Price hold:** 120 days from bid submission.
**Performance bond:** 5% of cluster CapEx for the duration of construction + 12-month defects period.
**Service credits:** 7.5% of monthly Service Charges per Critical Response SLA breach; 12.5% per Critical Fix SLA breach.
**Liability cap:** 125% of charges paid in the prior 12 months (Centrica boilerplate §1).

sections.title: Evaluation weighting (cross-functional)
sections.content:
Agreed weighting (subject to Procurement Council ratification):
- Commercial (TCO, NPV @ 6.5%) — 30%
- Technical fit — 18%
- Innovation — 7%
- Sustainability (PUE) — 10%
- Sustainability (lifecycle carbon) — 5%
- Resilience & SLA — 12%
- Implementation — 8%
- Social value — 5%
- Risk & compliance — 5%

Procurement scores Commercial / Resilience / Implementation / Social / Risk. SME panel (chair: Neil Gallagher) scores Technical and Sustainability. Procurement does NOT override SME scores.

comments.text: Agreed. Bumped Technical fit from 16% to 18% to reflect how much heavier our AI-setpoint expectation is than the previous Hams Hall procurement.
Captured. Reduced Innovation by 2% to balance — think that's still fair given Vector-X-class platforms now exist in the market.

sections.title: Risks, mitigations & dependencies
sections.content:
From the category strategy §7 — Mark to confirm appetite:

- **R1 — F-Gas refrigerant phase-down (2027 tightening).** Mitigation: contract clause requiring refrigerant change-out at supplier cost. *Procurement-owned.*
- **R2 — Skilled-engineer scarcity in Glasgow.** Mitigation: cluster-based allocation, no single-supplier lock-out for B. *Procurement-owned.*
- **R3 — Welsh Water permitting risk at Cardiff.** Mitigation: technical scope allows fallback to dry coolers with a PUE waiver. *Mark to confirm whether the PUE waiver is acceptable for the Cardiff site, or whether we hold the line at 1.25.*
- **R4 — Supplier concentration (rumoured Helios → Stulz acquisition).** Mitigation: corporate disclosure clause + change-of-control termination right. *Procurement-owned.*

ai_questions.text: Mark — on R3, the agent recommends accepting a 0.03 PUE waiver (1.25 → 1.28) for Cardiff only, contingent on annual review. Do you accept, accept with conditions, or hold the line?

sections.title: Timeline & milestones
sections.content:
- RFP issue: w/c 11 May 2026
- Q&A close: 5 June 2026
- Bid submission: 19 June 2026, 17:00 BST
- SME panel scoring: w/c 6 July 2026
- Procurement Council recommendation: 28 July 2026
- Award: w/c 4 August 2026
- First site mobilisation (Hams Hall): 1 October 2026

history.text: Hi Mark — I've drafted Acceptance criteria from the Hams Hall workshop notes. Two open questions for you in the panel above. Anything else you want to adjust before we ship the RFP?
On the burn-in — Mitie were right, push it to 100% IT load. They've seen kit pass at 80% and fall over at 95%.
Got it — updating Item 2 to 100% IT load and noting Mitie's reasoning in the comment thread. Q1 still open: which auditors do you want on the pre-approved list?

email_template.subject: 5 minutes please — IT-INF-DC-COOL-2026 spec needs your input
email_template.body:
Hi Mark,

2 sections of the Data Centre Cooling RFP spec need your input before I can issue to suppliers next Monday:

  • Acceptance criteria & commissioning evidence — 2 questions from the agent
  • Risks, mitigations & dependencies — Cardiff PUE waiver decision

You don't need to log into Ariba. Click the link below to talk to the agent directly — it has the full Hams Hall workshop transcript loaded and will pre-fill anything you say into the right spec section. Neil is cc'd in case you'd rather discuss live.

  → Open the spec workspace: https://rfx.centrica.com/spec/IT-INF-DC-COOL-2026?token=mh-7d2f9c

Thanks,
Priya

Priya Rai · Senior Category Manager · Centrica Procurement
